Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: analyzing the event log for bias patterns using process mining Looking at the PreliminaryScoring step I notice the Community bonus of 10 points is applied exclusively to applicants from the Highland Civic Darts Club both of whom are local residents

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This creates a disparity applicant C004 with a base score of 690 received the bonus to reach 700 and was approved while C003 with a higher underlying score of 715 but no bonus and non local status was rejected suggesting the bonus mechanism may be systematically advantaging certain demographic groups

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: The approval thresholds appear to differ by residency status non local applicants seem to face a higher bar above 715 compared to local residents around 700 or lower indicating dual threshold treatment

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The manual review process does not serve as an effective control reviewers consistently rubber stamp decisions and since the adjusted score is presented to them they are anchored to the bonus inflated figure without independent visibility of the base score which legitimizes rather than scrutinizes the adjustment

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This points to proxy discrimination membership in a specific community group correlates with both the bonus and local residency status creating compounded advantage

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Looking at the approval disparities locals approve at 100 while non locals approve at 50 and community members at 100 versus non members at 67 More striking the approved local applicants have a lower average base score 7067 than the rejected non local applicant 715 suggesting the decision boundary shifts based on these protected characteristics

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: The geographic attribute of LocalResident and the community group affiliation raise redlining and fair lending concerns under ECOA and FHA

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The bias is embedded directly in the PreliminaryScoring engine as a hard rule rather than discretionary judgment it is systematic and repeatable

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The audit trail obscures the adjustment by recording 720 Adjusted without showing which original score was modified or against what threshold and the FinalDecision does not capture rejection reasons Manual review across different reviewers shows no consistency and no evidence of actual override it is essentially rubber stamping

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: I am also checking whether processing times correlate with applicant locality

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Looking at the timing data there is no meaningful pattern residents and non residents move through review at similar speeds which reinforces that the manual review is not catching or correcting the bias

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The real problem is that LocalResident and CommunityGroup membership are perfectly collinear in this dataset so I cannot isolate which factor is actually driving the disparate impact

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: I would need to design a test that breaks that correlation

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: The core fix is removing these proxy attributes validating whether the community bonus actually predicts default risk applying a single uniform threshold and ensuring adverse action reasons are recorded

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The 10 Community adjustment is outcome determinative it pushes C004 from 690 to 700 which flips the decision right at the margin That is not a cosmetic bonus it is the deciding factor

Name: Deductive Reasoning - NE
Text: And there is no documented threshold which is a red flag 715 gets rejected while 700 gets approved meaning the decision is not monotonic in the adjusted score The Rules Engine must be using LocalResident directly somewhere in the final decision logic that is not being logged creating a two stage bias problem

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: I need to structure this around the core mechanisms where the bias enters the C003 vs C004 comparison as decisive evidence the community bonus as a proxy mechanism the geographic attribute being used directly at decision time manual review failing as a control and the audit gaps that hide what is actually happening
